Facial Action Coding System represents human faces through a set of facial muscle movements called facial Action Units (AU).
In facial morphology, a muscle controls different AUs.
When showing a certain emotion, a group of AUs would be activated simultaneously.
Relations among AUs may be different in real and manipulated faces.
Defacto methods modify the entire face using graphical methods.
Defacto methods generate the individual facial regions by GAN.

node_2: average correlation intensity to other aus
edge: An AU in real face presents stronger average correlation intensity to other AUs.

node_2: average correlation intensity to other aus
edge: An AU in forgery face presents weaker average correlation intensity to other AUs.
The proposed Action Units Relation Learning framework aims to improve the robustness and generalization of the forgery detector by focusing on relations between local regions associated with AUs.
The AU Relation Transformer (ART) is a part of the proposed framework that explicitly learns the relations between AUs through a dual network structure.

The AU Relation Transformer (ART) works alongside Tampered AU Prediction (TAP) within the proposed framework to enhance the forgery detection by forming relations between AUs and predicting tampered regions.

The AU Relation Transformer (ART) consists of an AU-specific Branch for learning relations among AU-aligned regions, extracting embeddings aligned with individual AUs, and building their relations using attention mechanism.

Additionally, the AU Relation Transformer (ART) has an AU-agnostic Branch for learning relations among image-patch regions, constructed as a standard Vision-Transformer block [16] to establish relations between different image patches.

Tampered AU Prediction (TAP) introduces an auxiliary task of creating a Partial Face Mask to enhance the model's ability to detect local forgery defects by randomly removing AU-related regions from the facial area.

The Partial Face Mask generated by removing AU-related regions is utilized for modifying data in the remaining regions at both image and feature levels, enabling the model to predict the manipulated regions with the help of Local Tampering Supervision.
Local Tampering Supervision helps predict the manipulated regions in order to identify forgery images.
